mission.title: spire: Consider adding metadata to the entry that allows a registrar to self-identify
mission.description: Consider adding metadata to the entry that allows a registrar to self-identify. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current spire deployment
steps.description:
Verify your spire version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
helm list -n spire 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working spire installation.

steps.title: Review spire configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get configmap -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=spire
```
We've discussed this before but I failed to find the issue so I thought I'd open a new one to keep the conversation going.

SPIRE Controller Manager tries to be authoritative for the workload entries it manages.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Consider adding metadata to the entry that allows a
steps.description:
I'd wager generic entry metadata is likely out of scope as a solution for this request. Probably worth considering, but there has traditionally been a fair bit of opposition to the idea for a variety of reasons.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get events -n spire --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Consider adding metadata to the entry that allows a" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I'd wager generic entry metadata is likely out of scope as a solution for this request. Probably worth considering, but there has traditionally been a fair bit of opposition to the idea for a variety of reasons.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
